children.description: Board members are not contributing effectively, or group dynamics undermine productive governance. INCLUDES: passive or disengaged directors, rubber-stamping without genuine scrutiny, dominant personalities suppressing dissent, poor meeting discipline, inadequate preparation by members, factionalism within the board, avoidance of difficult conversations. EXCLUDES: lack of relevant expertise (Board Composition & Diversity Gaps), structural problems in governance design (Governance Framework & Structure), dysfunctional board-management interface (Board-Management Relationship).

children.label: Board-Management Relationship
children.description: The working relationship between the board and executive management is dysfunctional — either too close or too adversarial. INCLUDES: board micromanaging operations, board too deferential to management, information asymmetry exploited by management, unclear boundaries between governance and execution, CEO capturing the board, adversarial board-CEO dynamic, lack of constructive challenge. EXCLUDES: CEO's own leadership capability gaps (Executive Leadership Capability), board members' individual engagement levels (Board Engagement & Dynamics).

children.label: Governance Framework & Structure
children.description: The formal governance architecture — charters, committees, policies, and processes — is inadequate or poorly designed. INCLUDES: missing or ineffective board committees, unclear terms of reference, inadequate delegation frameworks, no board evaluation process, poor succession planning for board roles, outdated governance documents, absence of decision escalation protocols. EXCLUDES: regulatory compliance requirements (Risk & Compliance), how individual board members behave within the framework (Board Engagement & Dynamics), board-management interface problems (Board-Management Relationship).

children.label: Executive Leadership Capability
children.description: The senior leadership team lacks the capability or cohesion needed. INCLUDES: CEO/founder limitations, leadership team skill gaps, executive team dysfunction, poor decision-making quality, leadership transition challenges, founder-to-professional-management transition. EXCLUDES: mid-level leadership (Talent), board-level governance (Board Effectiveness).

children.label: CEO & Top Leader Limitations
children.description: The chief executive or top leader lacks the skills, judgment, or adaptability the organization's current situation demands. INCLUDES: CEO skill set mismatched to organizational stage, founder unable to scale beyond startup phase, leader's cognitive biases distorting strategy, inability to delegate, poor external representation, failure to evolve leadership style as context changes. EXCLUDES: dysfunction among the broader executive team (Executive Team Cohesion & Dynamics), skill gaps in specific functional leaders (Functional Leadership Gaps), leadership succession and transition challenges (Leadership Transition & Succession).

children.label: Executive Team Cohesion & Dynamics
children.description: The senior leadership team does not function effectively as a collective — misaligned, siloed, or in conflict. INCLUDES: executive team infighting, empire-building across divisions, lack of shared priorities, inability to have productive conflict, passive-aggressive dynamics, executives undermining peers, absence of collective accountability, leadership team that is a collection of individuals rather than a team. EXCLUDES: individual CEO limitations (CEO & Top Leader Limitations), missing functional capabilities (Functional Leadership Gaps), planned leadership changes (Leadership Transition & Succession).

children.label: Functional Leadership Gaps
children.description: Specific executive roles lack the expertise or judgment required for their functional domain. INCLUDES: CFO without experience at current organizational scale, CTO unable to evaluate emerging technology, CMO without relevant market expertise, leaders promoted beyond competence, functional leaders who are technically strong but strategically weak, missing executive roles that should exist given organizational complexity. EXCLUDES: CEO-specific limitations (CEO & Top Leader Limitations), interpersonal dynamics among executives (Executive Team Cohesion & Dynamics), mid-level management capability (Talent).

children.label: Leadership Transition & Succession
children.description: The organization is navigating or failing to plan for changes in senior leadership. INCLUDES: founder-to-professional-management transition, CEO succession without a pipeline, abrupt leadership departures with no continuity, new leader onboarding failures, cultural disruption during leadership change, interim leadership vacuum, resistance to handing over control. EXCLUDES: board succession planning as a governance structure (Board Effectiveness), individual leader skill gaps outside transition context (CEO & Top Leader Limitations, Functional Leadership Gaps).

children.label: Decision-Making & Accountability
children.description: The organization's decision-making processes are slow, unclear, lack accountability, or produce poor-quality decisions. INCLUDES: unclear decision rights, consensus paralysis, lack of accountability structures, poor information flow to decision-makers, over-centralization or excessive delegation, poor judgment and decision quality. EXCLUDES: strategic direction decisions (Strategy), financial decisions specifically (Financial).

children.label: Decision Rights & Authority Clarity
children.description: It is unclear who has the authority to make which decisions, leading to confusion, duplication, or paralysis. INCLUDES: overlapping mandates across roles, no documented decision authority matrix, ambiguous escalation paths, decisions that fall between organizational boundaries, multiple people believing they own the same decision, no one willing to claim ownership of a decision. EXCLUDES: decisions that are clearly owned but poorly executed (Decision Process & Speed), accountability after a decision is made (Accountability & Follow-Through).

children.label: Decision Process & Speed
children.description: Decisions take too long, involve too many people, or follow dysfunctional processes — even when authority is clear. INCLUDES: consensus paralysis, excessive committee layers, analysis paralysis, revisiting previously settled decisions, inability to decide under uncertainty, over-reliance on unanimous agreement, meeting-heavy decision culture that delays action. EXCLUDES: lack of clarity about who decides (Decision Rights & Authority Clarity), poor information reaching decision-makers (Decision Information Flow), failure to follow through after a decision is reached (Accountability & Follow-Through).

children.label: Decision Information Flow
children.description: Decision-makers do not receive the right information at the right time to make sound decisions. INCLUDES: data not surfaced to decision-makers, filtered or politicized reporting, information overload without synthesis, lack of feedback loops on decision outcomes, decision-makers insulated from operational reality, delayed or stale information. EXCLUDES: deliberately opaque information sharing for power reasons (Power Concentration & Transparency), the decision process itself (Decision Process & Speed), who holds decision authority (Decision Rights & Authority Clarity).

children.label: Accountability & Follow-Through
children.description: Decisions are made but not tracked, enforced, or owned — no one is held accountable for outcomes. INCLUDES: decisions made but never implemented, no tracking of decision outcomes, diffusion of responsibility, lack of consequences for missed commitments, no post-decision review, inability to distinguish decision failure from execution failure, culture of deniability. EXCLUDES: who has authority to decide (Decision Rights & Authority Clarity), the speed or process of reaching decisions (Decision Process & Speed).

children.label: Decision Quality & Judgment
children.description: INCLUDES: systematically poor decisions despite clear authority, adequate information, and functional processes; cognitive biases distorting organizational choices (anchoring, groupthink, sunk cost fallacy); failure to weigh evidence appropriately; pattern of decisions that are timely and well-owned but wrong; inability to calibrate risk appetite to context; decisions that optimize for short-term metrics at the expense of long-term outcomes; lack of frameworks or heuristics for recurring decision types. EXCLUDES: unclear authority over who decides (Decision Rights & Authority Clarity), slow or dysfunctional decision processes (Decision Process & Speed), inadequate information reaching decision-makers (Decision Information Flow), failure to implement decisions once made (Accountability & Follow-Through).

children.label: Power Concentration & Transparency
children.description: Power is concentrated in ways that undermine governance, or information flows are opaque. INCLUDES: founder entrenchment, dual-class share structures that disenfranchise the board, selective information sharing, organizational opacity, key-person dependency at executive level, informal power structures that bypass formal governance, lack of transparency to stakeholders. EXCLUDES: over-centralization of routine decisions (Decision-Making & Accountability), CEO capability limitations (Executive Leadership Capability), board composition problems (Board Effectiveness).

children.label: Structural Power Entrenchment
children.description: Formal mechanisms entrench power in ways that resist accountability or challenge. INCLUDES: dual-class share structures, founder veto rights, constitutional or charter provisions that prevent removal, self-appointing board mechanisms, golden parachutes that discourage challenges, voting structures that concentrate control, legal or structural barriers to governance reform. EXCLUDES: informal influence that bypasses formal structures (Informal Power & Shadow Governance), information being withheld (Organizational Transparency & Information Asymmetry), over-reliance on specific individuals (Key-Person Executive Dependency).

children.label: Informal Power & Shadow Governance
children.description: Real decisions are made through informal channels that bypass formal governance structures. INCLUDES: inner circle making decisions before official meetings, back-channel influence by non-accountable advisors, family or personal relationships driving organizational outcomes, informal veto power held by individuals without formal authority, culture of pre-meetings that render official forums performative. EXCLUDES: formal structural mechanisms that entrench power (Structural Power Entrenchment), lack of transparency to external stakeholders (Organizational Transparency & Information Asymmetry), formal key-person dependencies (Key-Person Executive Dependency).

children.label: Key-Person Executive Dependency
children.description: The organization is critically dependent on one or a few individuals at the executive level, creating fragility and power asymmetry. INCLUDES: single individual holding irreplaceable relationships or knowledge, organization unable to function without a specific leader, key-person risk used as leverage to avoid accountability, no succession depth for critical executive roles, institutional knowledge concentrated in one person. EXCLUDES: founder entrenchment through formal mechanisms (Structural Power Entrenchment), informal influence networks (Informal Power & Shadow Governance), mid-level key-person risk (Talent/Operations).

children.label: Organizational Transparency & Information Asymmetry
children.description: Information is selectively shared or systematically opaque, undermining stakeholder oversight and trust. INCLUDES: selective disclosure to board or investors, lack of transparency to employees about organizational direction, hidden financial arrangements, opacity in executive compensation, stakeholders unable to assess organizational health, management controlling the narrative through information gatekeeping, asymmetric access to performance data. EXCLUDES: poor information flow to decision-makers as a process problem (Decision-Making & Accountability), structural mechanisms that entrench power (Structural Power Entrenchment), informal power channels (Informal Power & Shadow Governance).

meta.audience: AI agent pre-analyzing organizations, then human reviewer before conversation
meta.leaf_level: Specific problem types recognizable in an org profile — concrete enough for an agent to match against observable signals, generic enough to apply across industries
